back_translation: And in the first year of Cyrus king of Persia, to fulfill the word of the LORD spoken through Jeremiah, the LORD stirred the spirit of Cyrus king of Persia to issue a proclamation throughout his kingdom and to record it in writing, saying:
“Cyrus king of Persia says thus: ‘The LORD, the God of heaven, has given me all the kingdoms on earth, and he has appointed me to build a house for him at Jerusalem in Judah.
Whoever among you is of his people, may his God be with him, and let him go up to Jerusalem in Judah and rebuild the house of the LORD, the God of Israel; he is the God who dwells in Jerusalem.
And everyone who remains, wherever he lives, let the people of that place help him with silver, gold, goods, and livestock, together with the freewill offering for the house of God at Jerusalem.’”

Then the heads of the families of Judah and Benjamin, together with the priests and the Levites — everyone whose spirit God had stirred — rose up and prepared to go up to rebuild the house of the LORD at Jerusalem.
And all those around them supported them with articles of silver, gold, goods, livestock, and valuables, besides all the freewill offerings.
King Cyrus also brought out the articles of the house of the LORD that Nebuchadnezzar had carried off from Jerusalem and placed in the temple of his gods.
Cyrus king of Persia had Mithredath the treasurer bring out those articles, and he counted them over to Sheshbazzar the prince of Judah.
This is the number of them: thirty gold basins, one thousand silver basins, twenty-nine knives,
thirty gold bowls, four hundred ten silver bowls of a second kind, and one thousand other articles.
All the articles of gold and silver were five thousand four hundred. Sheshbazzar brought all these up along with the exiles who returned from Babylon to Jerusalem.
